name: Embalse de Horno Tejero — return trip through the Tierra de Mérida County from Montijo - Vegas Bajas
name_original: Embalse de Horno Tejero — vuelta por la Comarca de Tierra de Mérida desde Montijo - Vegas Bajas
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Montijo are situated on the south bank of the Tagus River estuary in Portugal, offering diverse landscapes for cyclists. The region features predominantly paved surfaces, making it suitable for road cycling. While the immediate area around the Tagus Estuary is generally flat, longer routes extending from Montijo can incorporate more significant ascents, providing varied challenges. Best road cycling routes in Montijo The most popular road cycling route is Embalse de Horno Tejero — return trip through the Tierra de Mérida County from Montijo - Vegas Bajas, a 55.7 miles (89.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 40 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a notable elevation gain of 1,692 feet (516 meters). Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Los Milagros Aqueduct – Acueducto de los Milagros loop from Montijo - El Molino, a moderate 38.5 miles (61.9 km) path. This route offers a loop through the landscape with an ascent of 774 feet (236 meters). Local road cyclists also love the San Isidro in Pueblonuevo – Church of Saint Peter the Apostle loop from Montijo - El Molino, a 35.9 miles (57.9 km) trail leading through rural areas, often completed in about 2 hours 40 minutes. Road cycling around Montijo is defined by riverside areas, the Tagus Estuary Nature Reserve, and undulating asphalt roads. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy rides along flat terrain to more challenging routes with ascents. The routes in Montijo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 15 reviews. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Montijo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Montijo?
A: Montijo offers a diverse selection of over 20 road cycling routes, catering to various skill levels. These routes feature predominantly paved surfaces, making them ideal for road cyclists.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Montijo?
A: Yes, Montijo has several beginner-friendly road cycling routes. The immediate area around the Tagus Estuary is generally flat, offering gentle and relaxed rides perfect for those new to road cycling or looking for an easy excursion. There are 5 easy routes available.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Montijo?
A: Road cycling in Montijo offers varied terrain. While the areas along the Tagus Estuary are largely flat, providing scenic riverside rides, longer routes extending from Montijo can incorporate undulating asphalt roads and more significant ascents, offering a dynamic experience for those seeking a greater challenge.

Q: What are some scenic highlights or attractions I can see while road cycling in Montijo?
A: Many routes offer beautiful views of the Tagus Estuary Nature Reserve, known for its marshes and rich biodiversity, including large flamingo populations. You can also encounter historical charm with old tide mills along the riverfront. Specific viewpoints include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5116836" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Cerro de San Serván&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422547" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcony of Extremadura&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. While many routes are moderate, some longer excursions from Montijo can include more substantial ascents, providing a good challenge for experienced road cyclists. For instance, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991622507/embalse-de-horno-tejero-return-trip-through-the-tierra-de-merida-county-from-montijo-vegas-bajas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Horno Tejero — return trip through the Tierra de Mérida County from Montijo - Vegas Bajas&lt;/a&gt; covers 89.7 km with an elevation gain of 516 meters.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Montijo?
A: The road cycling routes in Montijo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 15 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from the flat estuary paths to more undulating roads, and the opportunities to explore charming villages and natural beauty.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Montijo?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Montijo are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. An example is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14656331" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Milagros Aqueduct – Acueducto de los Milagros loop from Montijo - El Molino&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 61.9 km with an ascent of 236 meters.

Q: What is the best season for road cycling in Montijo?
A: Montijo's climate makes it suitable for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and conditions, avoiding the peak heat of summer and the cooler, wetter days of winter. However, the region's mild climate means cycling is often possible even in winter.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites accessible by road bike in Montijo?
A: Yes, cycling routes around Montijo can lead through traditional villages, offering cultural immersion. Montijo itself has historical landmarks like the Cais Mill (a 17th-century tide mill) and the Fisherman's Museum. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5326703" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Saint Peter the Apostle&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of a popular loop route.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by water features or dams?
A: Yes, some routes offer views of significant water features. For example, you can cycle near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montijo Dam&lt;/a&gt;, and there are routes that provide &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6059684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;views of the Montijo Dam along the Guadiana River&lt;/a&gt;, offering picturesque scenery.

Q: Can I find less crowded road cycling routes in Montijo?
A: While popular routes can see more activity, the extensive network of over 20 routes means you can often find quieter paths, especially if you explore some of the less-trafficked rural roads or venture out during off-peak hours. The Tagus Estuary Nature Reserve also offers expansive areas where you can enjoy a peaceful ride.
tips: La Roca de la Sierra is a municipality in the province of Badajoz, located between Puebla de Obando and Villar del Rey. In the municipality there is a set of hills to the northwest of the town called Las Sierrillas.

The Horno Tejero Reservoir is a dam that collects the waters of the Sierra de San Pedro and from where you can see the adjacent town, Cordobilla de Lácara. It is located between the towns of Cáceres and Mérida.
Spectacular church located in the old center of the city of Montijo. The space that surrounds it is always full of people and with atmosphere.

waypoints.name: Horno Tejero Reservoir (views of Cordobilla de Lácara)
